QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_26

question.text: What is the NAV publication deadline for Fund of Funds (FoF) and overseas fund schemes?

answer.text: 9 AM on the next business day (T+1)
12 noon on the next business day (T+1)
10 AM on the next business day (T+1)
11 PM on the same business day (T-day)

explanation.text: **The Logic:** FoF and overseas fund schemes get an extended deadline of 10 AM on T+1 because their underlying funds may have different NAV computation timelines, especially overseas funds in different time zones. **The Trap:** The standard 11 PM T-day deadline does not apply to FoFs and overseas funds. The extended deadline acknowledges the practical challenge of valuing underlying funds across time zones. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series V-A covers FoF structures. The delayed NAV publication also impacts applicable NAV calculations for transactions in these schemes. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Clients investing in FoFs or international funds should understand that their transaction NAV depends on underlying fund valuations from different markets. This can cause a day's delay in NAV availability.
